mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.5pp, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and corporate revenue shocks, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes outside late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate corporate revenue expectations and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day market direction.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 3.0 percent year-over-year forces central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses statistical significance when CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production indicates demand outpacing supply capacity, creating inflationary pressure that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series, or 20-day returns are positive when GDP growth exceeds industrial production growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with falling unemployment during economic recovery phases signals strong labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of recovery-phase labor tightening episodes, or the joint signal underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark.
